_meta.title: Scale Image to Total Pixels Adv

inputs.exclude_tags: comic, greyscale, monochrome, text, letter, signature, watermark, page number, text focus, book, magazine, editorial, framework, border，1girl, solo, human, person, model, face, woman, lady, girl

inputs.prompt:
(NO TEXT, NO LETTERS, NO NUMBERS, ABSOLUTELY NO HUMANS, NO MODELS, NO SKIN), act as a luxury textile cinematographer for a high-end botanical editorial.
[Color & Tone]: Use the "Fresh Mint Green" from the reference images. The tone should be bright, high-key, and radiate a clean, cooling energy.
[Texture-Driven Composition]: The material has a rhythmic vertical ribbing. Arrange the fabric in a "Dynamic Geometric Flow": tilt the grain so the ribs run diagonally at 45 degrees. Create crisp but soft-edged architectural folds that allow the vertical lines to intersect and layer, creating a sense of sophisticated depth.
[Wraparound Soft Lighting]: REPLACE harsh directional light with "Luminous Volumetric Diffusion." Imagine light passing through a fine mist. There should be NO sharp, dark shadows. All shadows within the ribbed grooves must be soft, hazy, and pale, gently defining the texture without any "bite" or aggressive contrast.
[Tactile Realism]: Avoid a digital CGI look. Ensure the fabric has a "Velvety Matte" soul. Every single vertical ridge should show the soft tactile haze of fine cotton fibers (毛羽), giving it a gentle, fuzzy halo that makes it look exceptionally comfortable and premium.
[Final Goal]: A serene, high-fidelity macro close-up that is bright, airy, and feels 100% like a real, professional photograph designed for visual comfort.
